why: Happy path: the six REQUIRED fields (id/source/type/timestamp/sovereignty/payload) and nothing else. The baseline every conformant reader MUST accept.
A federated envelope with a single ed25519 origin stamp validates. Pins the stamp SHAPE (method/identity/signature>=88/at/role) — signing/verification bytes are RFC-0004's concern, not this vector's.
The hub-stamp method carries the extra REQUIRED stamped_by DID. Pins the second oneOf branch of signedByStamp and a 2-stamp chain.

MASKING CASE. `acme.monitor.prod-01` is a legacy `org.agent.instance`-shaped address that is COINCIDENTALLY exactly 3 segments, so it still validates under the fixed-3 grammar (myelin#183). A party whose legacy source happened to have 3 segments sees no breakage — which is exactly why the stale {2,4} prose in docs/envelope.md:123 escapes notice. Guards the drift the loose prose invites.
spec_version is an OPTIONAL, SIGNABLE integer; 3 is the current grammar. Present-and-current MUST validate.

WARN-ON-NEWER. A verifier MUST NOT reject an envelope solely because spec_version exceeds the version it knows (it SHOULD warn). This rule lives only in reference code (src/envelope.ts:277-290) and is inexpressible in JSON Schema, so cortex's Ajv stack has no such semantics — the OPEN DECISION on emission phasing (Phase 4b) tracks it.
The cross-field rule (distribution_mode in {direct,delegate} => target_assistant REQUIRED) is satisfied. Pins the allOf conditional.

economics and extensions are additionalProperties:true at every level and carry NO size bounds; arbitrary unknown content validates. These are UNSIGNED, MUTABLE channels on a signable envelope (Security Considerations: unauthenticated writable channels; the only control is a prose behavioural rule).

The pre-#31 single-object signed_by shim (not an array) is still ACCEPTED by the schema oneOf and the reference validator. This is an un-sunset migration window (OPEN DECISION: retirement release). Pairs with actor/shim-form-documented, which exposes the actor-resolution defect this form triggers.

data_residency is validated only as ^[A-Z]{2}$; the ISO 3166-1 REGISTRY is NOT enforced, so the ISO-unassigned code `XX` validates on a sovereignty-bearing routing input. docs/envelope.md additionally blesses the non-ISO regional code `EU`. What a sovereignty engine must do with an unassigned/regional code is undefined (OPEN DECISION: residency registry).

DIVERGENCE / collision pair with invalid.json envelope/timestamp-lowercase. myelin's ISO8601_RE does NO calendar-range check, so month 02 day 30, hour 25, minute/second 99 all parse and this ACCEPTS; cortex's ajv-formats full-mode date-time REJECTS it. Pins the CURRENT myelin reference on a SIGNABLE field. If the datetime-semantics OPEN DECISION selects RFC 3339 calendar validity, this vector moves to invalid.json.

originator is the SIGNABLE policy-attribution claim (myelin#160): the signer (signed_by[0]) differs from the actor (originator.identity). Pins the required {identity, attribution} shape and additionalProperties:false.
Actor resolution: when originator is present, its identity wins over the signing chain. The signer (did:mf:andreas-meta-factory) is NOT the actor.
Actor resolution: no originator => fall back to the FIRST stamp's identity (chain origin).

DEFECT-CATCHER. docs/envelope.md and namespace.md specify actor = signed_by[0].identity when no originator is present. But the reference getActorIdentity treats a non-array (shim-form) signed_by as an empty chain and returns undefined (CONFIRMED defect envelope/getactoridentity-drops-shim-form-actor). This vector pins the DOCUMENTED contract and MUST fail an implementation that reproduces the bug — a policy engine following the docs otherwise silently loses attribution for a validly-signed envelope.
Actor resolution: an unsigned envelope with no originator has no actor. Returns null (undefined in the reference); a policy engine gets no attribution and MUST NOT fabricate one.
